content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed the iterative_minimal workflow addition against the FlowPreset contract and associated persistence/resume surfaces. The stop-condition (advance_iterative_loop_state), git-based diff fingerprint (with .ralph-burning excluded via pathspec plus filesystem fallback), sidecar payload persistence with raw-transcript recovery, and cross-scope config validation (max > 0, stable <= max, tolerant of malformed unrelated project configs) are all implemented coherently. Loop policy is frozen into IterativeImplementerState on the first iteration, so mid-run config drift cannot disrupt an active loop, and stage_target is recorded for resume. Journal events (started/completed/loop_exited) plus the snapshot update after every iteration give enough state to reconstruct progress via reconstruct_iterative_state_from_events; duplicate started events on crash are benign because only completed events drive the rebuild. The seq +=/-= pairing on append failure is symmetric and safe. resume_terminal_iterative_stage_result recovers from .parsed.json sidecars, with fallback to .raw via backend-specific parsers; the test suite covers sidecar persistence failure, iteration context propagation, stage_failed iteration identity, and raw-transcript recovery. No critical, high, or medium-severity correctness or safety findings warrant fix-now, planned-elsewhere, or new-bead amendments.

## Amendments

None.
